---
title: "about"
---

---
title: "About"
---

# About Me

I am a Statistics & Data Science student at UCLA with a strong interest in using data to uncover meaningful patterns, communicate insights clearly, and support decision-making. I enjoy working on projects that combine technical analysis with real-world relevance.

My academic background has given me experience with statistical modeling, regression, machine learning, data cleaning, visualization, and interpreting results for different audiences. I am especially interested in projects where data can be used to better understand behavior, trends, and outcomes.

## Education

**University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA)**\
B.S. in Statistics & Data Science\
Expected Graduation: Month Year

**Relevant Coursework** - Regression Analysis - Machine Learning / Data Analysis - Statistical Computing - Data Visualization - Linear Algebra - Probability / Mathematical Statistics

## What I’m Interested In

I’m especially interested in: - data analysis for real-world decision-making - machine learning and predictive modeling - data visualization and communication - interdisciplinary projects connecting data with social, business, or public issues

## Outside the Classroom

Outside of my coursework, I enjoy working on collaborative data science projects and helping others learn technical tools. As a workshop lead for the National Student Data Corps (NSDC), I have presented SQL and Tableau workshops to groups of students, focusing on making data tools more accessible and practical for beginners. These experiences have strengthened my communication skills and my interest in teaching and mentorship within technical communities.

I also enjoy exploring how data can be used to better understand real-world systems, particularly in areas such as media, technology, and social trends. Working on interdisciplinary projects with students from different fields has helped me develop both technical and collaborative problem-solving skills, Growing up in Los Angeles and speaking both English and Spanish has also shaped my interest in using data to analyze diverse communities and social issues.
